title: China-to-Antigua and Barbuda Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC and Power Quality — IEC 61000 / IEC 62920 Evidence for PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Antigua and Barbuda solar PV inverter projects should prepare electromagnetic compatibility and power-quality evidence suitable for ABBS, APUA, project-owner, lender, or engineer review. The practical IEC evidence set includes IEC 62920 for PV power-conversion equipment EMC and relevant IEC 61000-series tests for harmonic emissions, conducted and radiated emissions, immunity, voltage dips, surge, and flicker where applicable. Because Antigua and Barbuda is an island grid with tourism, hospital, telecom, and resort loads, project reviewers may scrutinise harmonics, nuisance tripping, weak-grid behavior, and surge resilience more closely than a basic product certificate.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — electromagnetic compatibility, harmonic current emissions, flicker, immunity, surge, and power-quality test methods where applicable
ABBS standards and conformity context for imported electrical equipment where applicable
APUA project-specific power-quality and connection review requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China PV inverter EMC evidence commonly relies on GB/T 37408-2021 and domestic CNAS-accredited laboratory reports. These reports may include EMC and electrical-performance tests aligned with China domestic requirements. They are useful technical background, but they are not automatic substitutes for IEC 62920 or IEC 61000 evidence requested by ABBS, APUA, a lender, or an international project engineer. EMC results obtained under China nominal 220/380 V, 50 Hz settings should be reviewed against Antigua and Barbuda's 230/400 V, 50 Hz site conditions.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC-related requirements
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: GB/T 37408 EMC evidence should not be presented as IEC 62920 or IEC 61000 compliance unless the report explicitly covers those IEC standards and the exact inverter model. Exporters should prepare IEC 62920 and relevant IEC 61000 reports from an accredited laboratory, confirm harmonic and power-quality limits with the APUA project engineer, and check surge, corrosion, and enclosure protection measures for coastal and hurricane-exposed installations.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent; mandatory where ABBS, APUA, the project owner, lender, or engineer requires IEC EMC and power-quality evidence for connection or procurement
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or certification-program dependent under China domestic PV inverter certification; not a standalone proof of Antigua and Barbuda IEC EMC acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC reports are engineering background only unless they explicitly include IEC 62920 and applicable IEC 61000 test evidence for the supplied model. Prepare an IEC EMC and power-quality package, confirm APUA project limits, and validate the inverter for Antigua and Barbuda's 230/400 V, 50 Hz island-grid conditions.

source.publisher: Antigua and Barbuda Bureau of Standards (ABBS)

rows.requirement: APUA Grid Connection — IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 Evidence and 230/400 V, 50 Hz Settings
rows.target_requirement: Grid-tied solar PV inverter deployment in Antigua and Barbuda is a project-level utility connection question with Antigua Public Utilities Authority (APUA), within the standards context of the Antigua and Barbuda Bureau of Standards (ABBS). Practical submissions should prepare IEC 62116 anti-islanding evidence, IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence, site protection settings, commissioning records, and project-specific APUA connection conditions. Antigua and Barbuda's low-voltage grid should be treated as 230/400 V at 50 Hz. The 50 Hz frequency aligns with China, but the voltage does not: China nominal is 220/380 V.
target_standards: APUA project-specific grid-connection and commissioning conditions for distributed or utility solar PV installations
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Antigua and Barbuda low-voltage grid design basis: 230/400 V, 50 Hz
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 for photovoltaic power-station connection to power systems, NB/T 32004 for PV grid-connected inverter technical specifications, and GB/T 37408-2021 for grid-connected inverter requirements. These documents are based on Chinese grid conditions and are not automatic evidence for APUA acceptance. China's nominal low-voltage grid is 220/380 V at 50 Hz, so only the frequency aligns with Antigua and Barbuda; the voltage differs and inverter firmware profiles, voltage trip thresholds, reconnection timing, and power-quality behavior should be revalidated for 230/400 V.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China GB/T and NB/T grid-connection documents do not substitute for APUA project connection acceptance. Exporters should prepare IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 reports for the exact inverter model or family, document the Antigua and Barbuda country firmware profile, validate operation at 230/400 V and 50 Hz, and obtain APUA project-specific connection and commissioning acceptance. Do not state that Antigua and Barbuda voltage matches China; only the 50 Hz frequency aligns.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory at project level where APUA requires grid-connection review, utility acceptance, or commissioning before energisation
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China domestic grid or certification expectations only; it is not transferable to APUA Antigua and Barbuda acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat APUA grid connection as a separate project approval gate. Submit IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence, validate firmware for 230/400 V at 50 Hz, and document commissioning settings for the actual Antigua and Barbuda site. Chinese 220/380 V GB/T settings are not a voltage match and are not automatically accepted.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety and Island Deployment Durability
rows.target_requirement: Solar PV inverters supplied to Antigua and Barbuda should be documented against IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 safety requirements for power converters used in photovoltaic systems. ABBS standards review, APUA grid-connection review, project-owner specifications, and international lender procurement may also require evidence for outdoor enclosure rating, thermal derating, surge protection, corrosion protection, and installation suitability. Antigua and Barbuda's hurricane exposure, coastal salt-mist environment, and sea-freight supply chain through St John's make mechanical mounting, IP rating, anti-corrosion treatment, spare-parts planning, and packaging documentation practical safety and reliability issues.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
ABBS standards and conformity context for electrical products where applicable
APUA project-specific installation and commissioning acceptance where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter safety evidence often relies on GB/T 37408-2021 and NB/T 32004 test documentation, supported by CNAS-accredited laboratory reports and voluntary CQC / CGC certification where obtained. These Chinese documents are not automatically accepted as IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence for Antigua and Barbuda projects. China domestic test assumptions may also not cover Antigua and Barbuda's continuous marine humidity, salt mist, hurricane wind exposure, or higher 230/400 V nominal grid voltage.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China GB/T / NB/T safety evidence should be supplemented with IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates for the exact inverter model or family. For Antigua and Barbuda, exporters should also document environmental derating, corrosion resistance, surge protection, mounting integrity, and spare-parts / warranty support for hurricane-prone coastal operation. Sea-freight through St John's can add packaging, corrosion-prevention, and customs-documentation requirements that are not addressed by a China domestic inverter certificate.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but commonly mandatory in practice where ABBS, APUA, project owners, insurers, or lenders require IEC safety evidence and site-suitability documentation
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or certification-program dependent under China domestic PV inverter certification; GB/T and NB/T evidence is not automatically accepted as IEC 62109 evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A China GB/T or NB/T inverter file should not be treated as sufficient IEC 62109 safety evidence for Antigua and Barbuda. Prepare IEC 62109-1/-2 certificates, confirm ABBS and APUA project documentation needs, and document hurricane, salt-mist, surge, mounting, and logistics controls before shipment through St John's.

source.publisher: Antigua and Barbuda Bureau of Standards (ABBS)
